_note: laptop-scale validation (6-core injector on a 14-core contended Mac, short holds) — proves the COMPLEX templated-POST scenario renders per request with ZERO connection/throttle errors at every point (the Envoy Lua body-drain fix; without it a POST-with-body collapsed at ~57% kind=connection errors). Same contract as inject-ceiling.json. Per-point injector_cpu_pct is a single docker-stats snapshot and is NOISY on a contended laptop, so do NOT derive rps/core from a single point here. A CONTROLLED fixed-2000-rps averaged-CPU measurement on this same machine gave simple=26% vs complex=39% CPU -> the complex request costs ~1.5x the CPU per request (complex rps/core < simple rps/core), the expected direction. CI's idle fat 8-core injector produces the real plateau + a clean rps/core factor.
